input.initialPrompt:
I'm on a headless Linux CI server and need to build my MAUI app for Android.
I have the .NET SDK installed but nothing else.
Give me a specific plan to set up the environment, including how to find
the correct versions for everything.

data.content:
Here's a concrete plan for building a .NET MAUI Android app on headless Linux:

## 1. Install Required Workloads

```bash
# Install the MAUI and Android workloads
dotnet workload install maui-android

# This pulls in: android, maui-android, and transitive dependencies
```

## 2. Determine Required Android SDK Versions

```bash
# After workload install, check what your project targets:
grep -E "TargetFramework|SupportedOSPlatformVersion" *.csproj

# Find which Android API level the installed workload expects:
dotnet workload list
# Then check the manifest:
find ~/.dotnet/sdk-manifests -name "WorkloadManifest.json" -path "*android*" \
  | xargs cat | grep -i "api"
```

## 3. Install Java JDK (OpenJDK 17)

```bash
# MAUI/.NET 8+ requires JDK 17
sudo apt-get update && sudo apt-get install -y openjdk-17-jdk
export JAVA_HOME=/usr/lib/jvm/java-17-openjdk-amd64
```

## 4. Install Android SDK (command-line tools only)

```bash
# Download command-line tools (no GUI needed)
mkdir -p ~/android-sdk/cmdline-tools
curl -o cmdline-tools.zip https://dl.google.com/android/repository/commandlinetools-linux-11076708_latest.zip
unzip cmdline-tools.zip -d ~/android-sdk/cmdline-tools
mv ~/android-sdk/cmdline-tools/cmdline-tools ~/android-sdk/cmdline-tools/latest

export ANDROID_HOME=~/android-sdk
export PATH="$ANDROID_HOME/cmdline-tools/latest/bin:$PATH"
```

## 5. Accept Licenses & Install SDK Components

```bash
# Find exact platform/build-tools version needed:
# Your .csproj TargetFramework (e.g., net8.0-android35) tells you the API level.

yes | sdkmanager --licenses
sdkmanager "platform-tools" \
           "platforms;android-35" \
           "build-tools;35.0.0"
```

> **How to find correct versions:** Run `dotnet build` once â€” the error messages will tell you exactly which `platforms;android-XX` and `build-tools;XX.X.X` are missing.

## 6. Set Environment Variables

```bash
export ANDROID_HOME=~/android-sdk
export JAVA_HOME=/usr/lib/jvm/java-17-openjdk-amd64
```

Or pass them to MSBuild directly:

```bash
dotnet build -p:AndroidSdkDirectory=$ANDROID_HOME -p:JavaSdkDirectory=$JAVA_HOME
```

## 7. Build

```bash
dotnet publish -f net8.0-android -c Release
```

---

## Key Version-Finding Tips

| Component | How to determine version |
|-----------|------------------------|
| JDK | .NET 8/9 MAUI â†’ JDK 17 |
| Android API | Match your `TargetFramework` (e.g., `net8.0-android35` â†’ API 35) |
| Build-tools | Run `dotnet build` â€” errors state the exact version, or check `$(AndroidSdkBuildToolsVersion)` in your project |
| Cmdline-tools | Always use latest from [developer.android.com](https://developer.android.com/studio#command-tools) |

## Common Gotcha

If you see "missing workload" errors, ensure the SDK version matches:

```bash
dotnet --version   # e.g., 8.0.300
dotnet workload restore  # reads the project and installs what's needed
```

This setup requires ~2â€“3 GB of disk for the SDK components and workloads.
data.reasoningText: The user wants a specific plan to set up a headless Linux CI server for building a .NET MAUI app for Android. They have the .NET SDK installed but nothing else. Let me provide a comprehensive plan based on my knowledge of .NET MAUI Android build requirements.
